title: Selaginella myosurus
taxonomy: Selaginella myosurus is classified within the family Selaginellaceae and is a member of the order Selaginellales. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The plant's classification also includes the kingdom Plantae, indicating its status as a flowering plant or a non-vascular plant, depending on the specific characteristics of the species. Further classification places it within the class Lycopodiopsida, a group of vascular plants that are thought to be among the most ancient living plants. At the species level, Selaginella myosurus is a unique member of its genus due to its distinct characteristics and adaptations.
uses: Selaginella myosurus is known for its traditional medicinal uses in some parts of the world, particularly in Asia and Africa. It is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are often used to make tea or infusions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, Selaginella myosurus is also used to treat digestive problems and as a natural remedy for respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of its medicinal uses.
